headline: Winners And Losers Of Q4: SLB (NYSE:SLB) Vs The Rest Of The Oilfield Services Stocks
summary: Quarterly earnings results are a good time to check in on a company’s progress, especially compared to its peers in the same sector. Today we are looking at SLB (NYSE:SLB) and the best and worst performers in the oilfield services industry.

headline: Iran’s Ceasefire Sends Energy Lower. Why Oil Services Stocks Are Holding Up.
summary: Companies like SLB and Baker Hughes should benefit from postconflict repairs to oil infrastructure and increased output.

headline: SLB Lands Major Deal To Help Tap Suriname's Oil Reserves Faster
summary: SLB enters long-term collaboration with PETRONAS Suriname, aiming to co-develop cost-efficient subsea solutions in a frontier basin.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider SLB (SLB) After Its 62% One-Year Rally?
summary: Investors may be wondering if SLB at around US$50 a share still offers value, or if most of the opportunity is already reflected in the price. The stock last closed at US$50.35, after a 2.0% decline over the past week, a 7.4% gain over the past month, a 25.2% return year to date, and a 62.4% return over the last year. Recent attention on energy services and oilfield activity has kept SLB in focus for investors who are assessing how the company fits into broader energy themes. Market...

headline: How Investors Are Reacting To SLB (SLB) Deepening Its Suriname Subsea Collaboration Strategy
summary: SLB’s OneSubsea unit previously signed a long-term collaboration with PETRONAS Suriname E&P B.V. and Subsea7’s Subsea Integration Alliance to provide integrated subsea engineering, procurement, construction, installation, and commissioning solutions across multiple discoveries in Suriname’s frontier basin. This agreement underlines SLB’s push to embed itself early across the offshore project lifecycle with partners, aiming to co-create lower-cost, subsea-led field developments in emerging...
